title: Chrysojasminum bignoniaceum
intro: Chrysojasminum bignoniaceum, also known as the yellow jasmine, is a flowering plant native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its fragrant, yellow flowers and twining habit. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Chrysojasminum bignoniaceum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are valued for their ability to promote emotional balance and well-being.
taxonomy: Chrysojasminum bignoniaceum is classified as a flowering plant in the family Oleaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Chrysojasminum, which is closely related to Jasmine and other flowering plants. Chrysojasminum bignoniaceum is often referred to as Bignonia jasmine or Golden Jasmine due to its golden yellow flowers. The plant is native to tropical regions and is widely cultivated for its fragrant and ornamental value. Chrysojasminum bignoniaceum is also known to be a popular choice for landscaping and ornamental gardens.

The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. Its extracts have also been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, making it a potential natural remedy for certain diseases.
